---
title: "BrainAlign Runbook"
output: github_document
---

# Purpose

This runbook explains the order to run the consolidated scripts on the training PC.

All user-facing shell / PowerShell / Python entry points now live in `scripts/`.
The notebooks remain for exploratory analysis only.

# One-Time Setup

From the repo root:

```bash
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
python -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

# Optional Sanity Checks

These are lightweight and safe to run before training:

```bash
python scripts/test_model.py
python scripts/print_shapes.py
python scripts/print_fmri_struct.py
python scripts/test_meg_metadata.py
python scripts/test_pd.py
```

Windows PowerShell uses the same Python commands after activation.

# Recommended Run Order On The PC

## 1. Verify the shared image list

If `data/shared_images.txt` is already correct, keep it. If you need to regenerate it, run:

```bash
python scripts/build_shared_images.py
```

This script ignores unmapped MEG event IDs such as button-press artifacts and rewrites `data/shared_images.txt`.

## 2. Train or resume full-data retrieval models

These runs are for modality-to-image and image-to-modality retrieval.

### MEG full-data retrieval

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --resume
```

### fMRI full-data retrieval

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --resume
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -Resume
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -Resume
```

## 3. Evaluate full-data retrieval models

Run this after the full-data checkpoints exist.

### MEG retrieval

Example for subject 1:

```bash
python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality meg \
  --subject 1 \
  --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub01_best.pt \
  --split test
```

### fMRI retrieval

Example for subject 3:

```bash
python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality fmri \
  --subject 3 \
  --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub03_best.pt \
  --split test
```

Outputs are written under `results/meg/` and `results/fmri/`.

If you are using PowerShell on the PC, the same `python scripts/...` evaluation commands work after activating the virtual environment.

## 4. Train or resume shared-only conversion models

These runs are only for cross-modality conversion on the shared image intersection.

### MEG shared-only conversion model

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --shared-only --resume
```

### fMRI shared-only conversion model

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --shared-only --resume
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -SharedOnly -Resume
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -SharedOnly -Resume
```

Shared-only checkpoints are saved with `_shared` in the filename so they do not overwrite the full-data retrieval checkpoints.

## 5. Evaluate shared-only retrieval models

This is optional but useful to confirm the shared-subset models are healthy before conversion.

```bash
python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality meg \
  --subject 1 \
  --shared-only \
  --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub01_shared_best.pt \
  --split test
```

```bash
python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality fmri \
  --subject 3 \
  --shared-only \
  --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub03_shared_best.pt \
  --split test
```

## 6. Run modality-conversion evaluation

Once both shared-only checkpoints exist:

```bash
python scripts/evaluate_conversion.py \
  --source-modality meg \
  --source-subject 1 \
  --source-ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub01_shared_best.pt \
  --target-modality fmri \
  --target-subject 3 \
  --target-ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub03_shared_best.pt \
  --split test
```

This writes results under `results/conversion/`.

# Suggested PC Execution Order

Run these in this order:

1. `python scripts/test_model.py`
2. `python scripts/print_shapes.py`
3. `python scripts/build_shared_images.py` if `data/shared_images.txt` needs to be refreshed
4. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --resume`
5. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --resume`
6. `python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality meg --subject <id> --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub<id>_best.pt --split test`
7. `python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality fmri --subject <id> --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub<id>_best.pt --split test`
8. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --shared-only --resume`
9. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --shared-only --resume`
10. `python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality meg --subject <id> --shared-only --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub<id>_shared_best.pt --split test`
11. `python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality fmri --subject <id> --shared-only --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub<id>_shared_best.pt --split test`
12. `python scripts/evaluate_conversion.py --source-modality meg --source-subject <meg_id> --source-ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub<meg_id>_shared_best.pt --target-modality fmri --target-subject <fmri_id> --target-ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub<fmri_id>_shared_best.pt --split test`

If the PC is running Windows PowerShell, replace steps 4, 5, 8, and 9 with:

1. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -Resume`
2. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -Resume`
3. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -SharedOnly -Resume`
4. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -SharedOnly -Resume`

# Notes

- EEG checkpoints are already present and can still be evaluated separately.
- Full-data retrieval and shared-only conversion are intentionally separate workflows.
- The conversion evaluator currently assumes shared-image alignment and aggregates embeddings per `image_id`.
